reasoning: The Lean statement reframes "connected spaces X, Y with proper subsets A, B" as "arbitrary topological spaces X, Y with connected subsets A₂, B₂ and proper subsets A₁ ⊊ A₂, B₁ ⊊ B₂", with conclusion on (A₂ ×ˢ B₂) \ (A₁ ×ˢ B₁). These are logically equivalent: taking A₂ = univ, B₂ = univ recovers the informal statement (since IsConnected univ ↔ ConnectedSpace), and conversely the Lean statement follows from the informal one by applying it to the subspace topologies on A₂ and B₂ (since subspace topology commutes with products). The set-builder {x | ∃ a b, x = (a,b) ∧ a ∈ A₂ ∧ b ∈ B₂} is exactly A₂ ×ˢ B₂, and ⊂ in Mathlib is proper subset. No hypotheses are silently added or dropped.
